name: Telangana
stopoverNote: Hyderabad Rajiv Gandhi International Airport (HYD) is the natural entry and exit hub; allow 45–60 minutes by cab between the airport and central Hyderabad, and use Secunderabad station for the fast train to Warangal.

diet.vegetarian: Hyderabadi cuisine is famous for meat-heavy biryani, haleem and nihari, but vegetarian travellers are well covered in Hyderabad with paneer or mushroom biryani, South Indian tiffin, Mirchi ka Salan, Bagara Baingan and veg chains such as Chutneys, Kamat and Bikanervala. In Warangal and Nagarjuna Sagar, stick to South Indian meals, tiffin and hotel restaurants for the safest veg choice.
diet.vegan: Vegan options are easiest in Hyderabad, where dedicated or vegan-friendly cafés exist, but Irani chai, sweets such as Qubani ka Meetha and double ka meetha, paneer dishes and many biryanis use dairy or ghee. Ask clearly for no ghee, butter, paneer, curd or milk; outside Hyderabad, carry snacks and choose idli, dosa, rice meals or simple dal made without ghee.
phrases: Neyyi, venna, paalu, perugu vaddu = no ghee, butter, milk or curd
Naaku shakaharam kavali = I want vegetarian food
Guddu kuda vaddu = no egg also

cities.name: Hyderabad
cities.notes: A city of Nizams and tech giants — Charminar bazaars, Golconda's ramparts, Qutb Shahi tombs, museums and biryani sit beside modern cafés and film-city spectacle.

cities.name: Warangal
cities.notes: The former Kakatiya capital — an open-air heritage circuit of fort gateways, Thousand Pillar Temple and Ramappa Temple, Telangana's UNESCO-listed masterpiece.

cities.name: Nagarjuna Sagar
cities.notes: A reservoir-and-ruins finale where Nagarjunakonda island preserves Buddhist stupas and museum treasures rescued from the valley before the dam filled.

itinerary.sim: Any Indian prepaid SIM from Jio or Airtel works well across Hyderabad, Warangal and the highway towns; carry offline maps for the Warangal→Nagarjuna Sagar road and around the dam area.

Hyderabad.name: Hyderabad
Hyderabad.note: Three days is ideal: one for Old City monuments and bazaars, one for the Golconda-Qutb Shahi cluster, and one full day for Ramoji Film City.

days.note: Start with the indoor museum, then walk the Charminar cluster after lunch; add the Golconda sound-and-light show only if you leave Old City by about 5:30pm.

activities.name: Salar Jung Museum
activities.tip: Closed Fridays; arrive near opening for the Veiled Rebecca and Tipu Sultan galleries.

activities.name: Charminar climb and arcade
activities.tip: Friday afternoons are crowded around prayers; keep valuables close in the bazaar lanes.

activities.name: Laad Bazaar bangles and Old City lanes
Mecca Masjid
activities.tip: Dress modestly; it sits beside Charminar.

activities.name: Chowmahalla Palace
activities.tip: Closed Fridays; pair it with Charminar and Laad Bazaar.

activities.name: Golconda Sound & Light Show
activities.tip: English show timing shifts by season; buy at the fort counter from late afternoon.

hotels.name: Banjara Hills or Jubilee Hills mid-range hotel

meals: Hotel Shadab or Nayaab for biryani/nihari
Chutneys or Kamat for vegetarian South Indian
Irani chai and Osmania biscuits near Charminar

days.note: Qutb Shahi Tombs and Golconda are under 2km apart, but the fort is large and hot; keep the afternoon flexible for Birla Mandir or Hussain Sagar.

activities.name: Qutb Shahi Tombs
activities.tip: Go early for cooler weather and better light on the restored domes.

activities.name: Golconda Fort
activities.tip: Wear grippy shoes; the acoustic clap at Fateh Darwaza is the classic guide demo.

activities.name: Birla Mandir and Planetarium
activities.tip: The temple terrace is best at sunset.

activities.name: Hussain Sagar Lake and Necklace Road

hotels.name: Banjara Hills or Jubilee Hills mid-range hotel

meals: Paradise or Café Bahar for biryani
Smart Alec or Terrassen Café for vegan-friendly options
Bikanervala for reliable vegetarian meals

days.note: Treat Ramoji as the only major plan for the day: it is 30km east of central Hyderabad, opens around 9am and usually fills the day until late afternoon.

activities.name: Ramoji Film City full-day tour
activities.tip: Book online and arrive at opening; Star Experience packages cost more and include added comforts.

activities.name: Live shows, gardens and film sets
activities.tip: Check show timings at the gate and prioritise 3–4 headline shows.

hotels.name: Banjara Hills or Jubilee Hills mid-range hotel

meals: Ramoji in-park restaurants
Return to Jubilee Hills for cafés
Veg biryani or South Indian tiffin near your hotel

Warangal.name: Warangal
Warangal.note: A packed but feasible one-day Kakatiya circuit if you pre-hire a cab for Ramappa; add a second day for Laknavaram Lake or slower temple time.

days.note: Start in Warangal by 8am for Thousand Pillar Temple and the fort, then drive 70km to Ramappa after lunch; it is tight but realistic with a pre-booked cab.

activities.name: Ramappa Temple (Rudreshwara), Palampet
activities.tip: UNESCO World Heritage Site; hire an on-site guide for the floating-brick and Madanika details.

activities.name: Warangal Fort and Kakatiya Kala Thoranam
activities.tip: Focus on the four gateways and central temple ruins if time is short.

activities.name: Thousand Pillar Temple
activities.tip: A 12th-century trikuta temple with Shiva, Vishnu and Surya shrines.

hotels.name: Mid-range hotel near Kazipet or Hanamkonda

meals: Simple South Indian meals in Hanamkonda
Carry snacks and water for the Ramappa drive
Hotel restaurant dinner near Kazipet

days.note: Use this only if you add a second night in Warangal; Laknavaram is about 85km away and does not fit comfortably into the packed UNESCO day.

activities.name: Laknavaram Lake and suspension bridge
activities.tip: Best after monsoon but check road conditions in heavy rain.

activities.name: Extra time at Ramappa Temple or local museums

meals: South Indian tiffin
Local thali
Tea and snacks near Hanamkonda

Nagarjuna Sagar.name: Nagarjuna Sagar
Nagarjuna Sagar.note: Two days lets you arrive from Warangal, see the dam and Ethipothala, then take the mandatory morning boat to Nagarjunakonda before returning to Hyderabad.

days.note: Leave Warangal by 9am and reach around lunch; keep the afternoon to dam viewpoints and Ethipothala so you are rested for the next morning's island boat.

activities.name: Nagarjuna Sagar Dam viewpoint
activities.tip: Golden hour is best; ask locally whether the crest walk is open.

activities.name: Ethipothala Waterfalls
activities.tip: Carry cash; the research found only unverified ₹10–₹20 references.

activities.name: Buddhavanam Buddhist Heritage Park
activities.tip: A useful backup if the island boat is unavailable.

meals: Haritha hotel restaurant
Simple South Indian meals near the dam
Carry snacks for limited late-evening options

days.theme: Nagarjunakonda island and return to Hyderabad
days.note: Buy boat tickets at the tourism counter by 9am and take the 9:30am sailing; after the island museum and ruins, depart by mid-afternoon for Hyderabad.

activities.name: Boat ride to Nagarjunakonda Island
activities.tip: Last practical boat is early afternoon; arrive early on weekends.

activities.name: Nagarjunakonda Archaeological Museum
activities.tip: Look for Buddhist sculpture and Ikshvaku-period artefacts.

activities.name: Island ruins walk — stupas, viharas and amphitheatre
activities.tip: Carry water and sun protection; shade is limited.

hotels.name: Return to Hyderabad hotel or airport hotel

meals: Early breakfast before the boat
Packed snacks for the island
Dinner back in Hyderabad

connections.method: Train from Secunderabad or AC cab
AC cab/self-drive via Jangaon, Nalgonda and NH 565
AC cab or TSRTC bus via Ibrahimpatnam and ORR

extras: Book Ramoji Film City online and keep it as a standalone Hyderabad day; it is not realistic to combine with Old City or Golconda sightseeing.
For Warangal, pre-arrange a cab for Ramappa Temple because public transport is unreliable for the 70km Palampet detour.
At Nagarjuna Sagar, buy Nagarjunakonda boat tickets early from the tourism counter, not at the jetty; boats can sell out or pause in rough monsoon weather.
Avoid April–June heat for forts and exposed ruins; July–September can disrupt Nagarjuna Sagar boat services and rural roads.
